name: Team Donnelly
subject: these numbers are shocking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|
---

| | Peter,

Our democracy was founded on the concept of one person, one vote. But politicians have used gerrymandering to rig the system against voters so that they can hold on to power.

Think about this: Hoosier Democratic candidates received roughly 40% of the votes for Congress in 2016, but hold just 2 of Indiana's 9 seats. **Those numbers are shocking, but the reason is simple: Too many politicians care more about winning elections than winning a battle of ideas.**

When elections get less competitive, people stop voting. No one knows that better than we do right here in Indiana where, in 2014, we had the lowest voter turnout of any state.

As people stop voting, special interests are assured that their bought politicians stay in office, even after voting to raise taxes on the middle class and strip millions of their health care.

**_We must reverse course on voter suppression tactics like gerrymandering, Peter._** Joe's fighting to expand voting rights in the Senate, but he wants to hear from you. **[Will you take this survey to let us know what you think about gerrymandering in Indiana?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2531/7377f838/35ac9b52/51fa5b72/2010699160/VEsH/ "https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/HMD9QRV")**

Hello,
This coming week could be make-or-break for hard-working families -- the tax giveaway Washington Republicans are pushing through the Senate is every bit as bad as you've heard.
It would:
* **Cut taxes for the rich. **The bill gives nearly 50% of tax cuts to the top 5% wealthiest households in the country in the first year of the law, and gives 98% of millionaires a tax cut compared to just 27% of middle class Americans.
* **Drive up health insurance premiums by 10%.** Middle class families across the country could see their premiums go up by $2,000 each year.
* **Slash Medicare funding by $25 BILLION. **This bill would rip coverage away from those with disabilities and the elderly - our friends and family members who need it the most.

This is a terrible bill for taxpayers like you, but far-right Republicans, desperate for a legislative win, are fighting tooth and nail to jam it through the Senate.

subject: Tell the Senate: Block the attack on abortion rights in the Trump tax scam
body:
Tell the Senate: Block the attack on abortion rights in the Trump Tax Scam (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/TaxScam_Personhood_dems)

Tell the Senate:
“Do everything in your power to block the anti-abortion fetal personhood language in the Trump Tax Scam.”

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/TaxScam_Personhood_dems)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/TaxScam_Personhood_dems)

Republicans attack abortion rights whenever and wherever they can. Right now, it is through the Trump Tax Scam.

It is not enough that the bills moving through Congress would make deep cuts to the social safety net and raise taxes on middle- and lower-income families – all while giving Trump and his wealthy friends trillions in handouts. They would also strike an intentional blow to abortion rights by enshrining the language “unborn child” into the federal tax code.1

Senate Democrats must push back on this attack on women with everything they’ve got. They should be calling out Republicans’ attempt to inject anti-abortion politics into the tax code and pledging to block any tax bill that contains the unborn child provision. If we speak out now, we can make sure this issue does not get lost among all the other horrible things in the tax scam. Can you add your name today?

Tell the Senate: Block the anti-abortion provision in the Trump tax scam. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/TaxScam_Personhood_dems)

Republican right-wing extremists in Congress are trying to sneak their unborn child language into a provision about the 529 plan, a college savings account. These accounts let parents put aside money for their children’s education. They already allow parents to set up accounts for their children before they are born. Parents set up the account in their own names and then transfer it to their child’s name when the child is born. Republicans’ new language specifically states that an “unborn child may be treated as a designated beneficiary or an individual under section 529 plans.”2 It is unnecessary as tax law, but a great way for right-wing Republican leadership to give a gift to the anti-abortion extremists in their base.

Enshrining “unborn child” in the tax code helps these extremists achieve their goal of legally establishing fetal personhood. If they can codify the notion that life begins at conception, they can claim that fetuses have constitutional rights and lay the groundwork for banning abortion outright. Republicans in states across the country have tried other ways to establish fetal personhood. Personhood amendments have failed in every state, but other attempts to give fetuses, and even embryos, rights or to punish women for endangering their fetuses have succeeded in greasing “an already slippery slope” of prioritizing fetal rights over women’s rights.3

Senate Democrats, along with any Republican with a conscience who cares more about women than party politics, must shut this latest attempt down. Help make sure they know we are expecting them to stand strong for women’s rights and block this right-wing extremist sneak attack on abortion. Click the link below to sign the petition:

https://act.credoaction.com/sign/TaxScam_Personhood_dems (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/TaxScam_Personhood_dems)

Thank you for fighting for abortion rights today,

Heidi Hess, Senior Campaign Manager
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/TaxScam_Personhood_dems)

References:

clean_body: Tell the Senate: Block the attack on abortion rights in the Trump Tax Scam () Tell the Senate: “Do everything in your power to block the anti-abortion fetal personhood language in the Trump Tax Scam.” Add your name: () Dear Activist, () Republicans attack abortion rights whenever and wherever they can. Right now, it is through the Trump Tax Scam. It is not enough that the bills moving through Congress would make deep cuts to the social safety net and raise taxes on middle- and lower-income families – all while giving Trump and his wealthy friends trillions in handouts. They would also strike an intentional blow to abortion rights by enshrining the language “unborn child” into the federal tax code.1 Senate Democrats must push back on this attack on women with everything they’ve got. They should be calling out Republicans’ attempt to inject anti-abortion politics into the tax code and pledging to block any tax bill that contains the unborn child provision. If we speak out now, we can make sure this issue does not get lost among all the other horrible things in the tax scam. Can you add your name today? Tell the Senate: Block the anti-abortion provision in the Trump tax scam. Click here to sign the petition. () Republican right-wing extremists in Congress are trying to sneak their unborn child language into a provision about the 529 plan, a college savings account. These accounts let parents put aside money for their children’s education. They already allow parents to set up accounts for their children before they are born. Parents set up the account in their own names and then transfer it to their child’s name when the child is born. Republicans’ new language specifically states that an “unborn child may be treated as a designated beneficiary or an individual under section 529 plans.”2 It is unnecessary as tax law, but a great way for right-wing Republican leadership to give a gift to the anti-abortion extremists in their base. Enshrining “unborn child” in the tax code helps these extremists achieve their goal of legally establishing fetal personhood. If they can codify the notion that life begins at conception, they can claim that fetuses have constitutional rights and lay the groundwork for banning abortion outright. Republicans in states across the country have tried other ways to establish fetal personhood. Personhood amendments have failed in every state, but other attempts to give fetuses, and even embryos, rights or to punish women for endangering their fetuses have succeeded in greasing “an already slippery slope” of prioritizing fetal rights over women’s rights.3 Senate Democrats, along with any Republican with a conscience who cares more about women than party politics, must shut this latest attempt down. Help make sure they know we are expecting them to stand strong for women’s rights and block this right-wing extremist sneak attack on abortion. name: The White House
subject: Tax reform in time for Christmas
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc4236d4135b0d1f9e4f6e6c2e2417e119ec5e91084597d15e50561269516de580f5acabce4c37eb7d42985624bfa67c06d10fc7e7274fb1

Tax reform in time for Christmas

With the holiday season upon us, President Donald J. Trump is working hard with Congress to cut taxes for middle-income families and fix our Nation's broken tax code in time for Christmas. The new bill – currently making its way through the Senate – will slash taxes by some $1.5 trillion over the next 10 years and is projected to boost household incomes significantly after nearly two decades of stagnation. The new code will allow families to spend more of their hard-earned dollars toward the success of their children, while restoring America's competitive business edge and stimulating job growth.

Today, the President is meeting to discuss the bill with Senators Orrin Hatch (R-UT), Pat Toomey (R-PA), Rob Portman (R-OH), Tim Scott (R-SC) and John Cornyn (R-TX) of the Senate Finance Committee. The Administration is pleased with the growing momentum behind passing these historic tax reforms, and it looks forward to continuing to support the Senate in getting this important work done as quickly as possible.

President visits with service members on Thanksgiving

On Thanksgiving Day, President Trump traveled to Station Lake Worth Inlet in Palm Beach County, Florida, to visit Coast Guard service members and hosted a video teleconference with U.S. military personnel on missions abroad. The President spoke with members of the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, and Coast Guard gathered on the video conference from bases in Afghanistan, Iraq, and Turkey, as well as from ships in the middle of the ocean. President Trump expressed his gratitude on behalf of the American people for these heroes’ dedication to our country.

“It’s an honor to speak with you all and to give God thanks for the blessings of freedom and for the heroes who really have this tremendous courage that you do to defend us and to defend freedom,” said the President.

Following the teleconference, President Trump visited with the Coast Guard and thanked them for their life-saving efforts following the recent hurricanes. “You saved a lot of people, and I just want to thank you on behalf of the whole country,” said President Trump.

See more from the visit
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc4236d4135b0d1fd5be3fb42248d6ee551384afb5cb72bb66b2f29dab3f859a144e89c0d766f53e4b8c7b9ef559679592d60f9ccea007b9
here.

Announcing Christmas 2017 at the White House

Today, First Lady Melania Trump officially unveils Christmas season at the White House. This year’s theme, “Time-Honored Traditions,” includes a tribute to service members with the Gold Star Family Tree, historic pieces celebrating the holiday tradition of dining and hospitality, and a nod to the first themed White House Christmas, the “Nutcracker Suite” in 1961. The State Dining Room holds the famous gingerbread house, which depicts the South facade of the White House and features Mrs. Trump’s signature Christmas wreaths.

“The President, Barron, and I are very excited for our first Christmas in the White House,” said Mrs. Trump. “As with many families across the country, holiday traditions are very important to us. I hope when visiting the People’s House this year, visitors will get a sense of being home for the holidays. On behalf of my husband and Barron, I want to wish everyone a Merry Christmas and joyous holiday season.”

Read more about Christmas at the White House 2017
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc4236d4135b0d1f1a7a1e9a03e63d85e8e357e94eab8b2b96b980ef0609f6ee70c2fe5de3a3261953fa36aeb08930b9fd08446e20c92411
here, and
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc4236d4135b0d1f09d47cdeb87b3bd4acb37f70be84e1050e4ab13821f661fdada6427871fcb0c340bf6c400bceeef6ab320efe9cf22398
watch First Lady Melania Trump unveil this year's Christmas decorations.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump visit the U.S. Coast Guard Station Lake Worth Inlet in Riviera Beach, Florida | November 23, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Today President Trump is having lunch with Vice President Mike Pence and members of the Senate Finance Committee before hosting an event in the Oval Office honoring Native American code talkers. Later in the afternoon, the President will meet with Secretary of Defense James Mattis.

Vice President Mike Pence participated in a meeting with King Abdullah II of Jordan this morning and later will join a meeting with the leadership of General Motors, Ford, and Fiat Chrysler.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, President Trump will visit the US Capitol to discuss tax reform with Senate GOP.

This week, Vice President Pence will deliver the keynote address at a celebration of the 70th anniversary of the historic United Nations vote calling for the establishment of a Jewish State. Meanwhile, Ivanka Trump is leading a U.S. delegation to the Global Entrepreneurship Summit in Hyderabad, India, to promote female entrepreneurship.

body:
Polls show that the Alabama Senate race is tightening -- and a recent Fox News poll even showed Doug Jones leading Roy Moore by 8 points.
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171127-Urgent_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Team --

The Alabama Senate race is going to come down to the wire.

Polls show that this race is tightening -- **Nate Silver 's _FiveThirtyEight_ says the race is tied and a recent _Fox News_ poll even showed Doug Jones leading Roy Moore by 8 points.**

It's clear Doug has the momentum -- but we can't let our feet off the gas this close to the finish line.

[**Give Doug the resources he needs to finish strong in Alabama. Contribute $1 right now to help us take on Republicans everywhere.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171127-Urgent_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

When this race started, pundits said there was no way a Democrat could win Jeff Session's seat, in a state that hasn't elected a Democratic Senator in two decades.

But with the help of supporters like you, Doug is running the strongest campaign possible -- and now, Alabama's three biggest newspapers are even calling on voters to reject Roy Moore.

Now Doug is counting on supporters like you to make the difference in the final weeks before Election Day.

**Keep up the momentum in Alabama. Pitch in $1 right now to fund our efforts as we fight for every state:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/down-to-the-wire**](http://action.dscc. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Congress returns today…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
The Republican tax plan amounts to a slow but sure destruction of the middle class.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTI0NDczMzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjQ5MjMyMwS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, The Senate returns to Congress today. If Mitch McConnell's plans go smoothly, Donald Trump's tax scheme could very well be brought to a full floor vote as early as Thursday. This is an all-hands-on-deck moment for our movement. The Republican tax plan amounts to a slow but sure destruction of the middle class. It targets middle-class homeowners, slashing deductions for mortgage interest and property taxes. This primarily affects states like Washington with higher property values. And of course, all that money they're "saving" by slashing middle-class tax breaks doesn't go to amping up public education or climate action programs. **It gives 1.5 trillion dollars of tax breaks to billionaires and corporations.** For example -- the bill enacts a special discount tax rate for people who own LLCs. Guess who owns multiple LLCs? Donald Trump. [**We can 't let this happen. I set a $25,000 end-of-month deadline to prove our strength as we fight Trump's disastrous agenda. Chip in $3 right now towards our official goal to fight against the Republican tax scheme.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0NDczMzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjQ5MjMyMwS2&mt=1&rt=0) It's bad enough that the tax bill will hike taxes on middle-class families, exacerbate homelessness, harm workers' retirement security, worsen student debt, and more. But it also guts Obamacare. **By repealing the individual mandate for health care coverage, 13 million fewer Americans are projected to have insurance in 2027** , and premiums are expected to rise for everyone. The America I believe in is not a country that rips away health care and hard-earned savings from tens of millions of Americans in order to give tax breaks to billionaires and corporations. The America I believe in supports those who pursue the American dream, who work hard for a living and save up to buy a home for their family. [**As we approach a likely vote at the end of November, it 's all the more important that we stand together. Chip in $3 now and join the fight to stop the Republicans' un-American tax scheme before it's too late.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0NDczMzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjQ5MjMyMwS2&mt=1&rt=0) I am deeply troubled by what the final GOP tax plan will mean for workers, families, and communities across Washington state. This partisan plan does not reflect our state's values, and it threatens our state's economy. The America I believe in works for all of us. I hope you'll be by my side in this fight to save the middle class. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MTI0NDczMzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjQ5MjMyMwS2&mt=1&rt=0)

body:
Our Revolution is thrilled to announce the opening of our official online store!
#
Friends,
The team at Our Revolution is thrilled to announce the opening of our official online store! Our merchandise is one hundred percent union-made and union-printed here in the United States. By purchasing some of our official merchandise, you're not only supporting union jobs but our grassroots organizing efforts across the country.

name: Peter
subject: they're going after health care *again*
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Did you see our note from this afternoon? I'm going to be honest, I can't believe I'm emailing you again about the Affordable Care Act. |
---

| | Peter,

Did you see our note from this afternoon? I'm going to be honest, I can't believe I'm emailing you _again_ about the Affordable Care Act. I guess the signs were all there -- after all, Republicans had voted on repealing the Affordable Care Act more than 50 times before the 2016 elections -- and it's not news that the GOP has been fixated on taking away healthcare from millions of Americans.

But after thousands of calls and letters to lawmakers, and after multiple polls demonstrating that repealing the ACA is disastrously unpopular, and even after three failed bills -- Republicans are trying to gut it again by adding an amendment to their tax bill.

**We 've got to stand strong to protect Hoosiers' health care. Donate today to show Joe you support his efforts against the GOP's latest attempt to dismantle the health care system.** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

****[Or, donate a different amount>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c253b/7377f838/35bdda87/51fa593d/1153757341/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-taxsurveykicker?refcode=20171127.2nd")****
Peter, here are the facts:
* **4 million fewer Americans would be covered** in the first year after the repeal took place. That number would rise to 13 million by 2027.
* Health care premiums would _rise by 10%_.
* Fewer younger, healthier Americans would sign up, skewing the insurance pool towards older, sicker citizens, **raising costs and reducing insurance options.**

Affordable, quality healthcare should not be a political issue. To thousands of Hoosier and American families, it's not. That's why even despite efforts to shrink the enrollment period and cut the budget for outreach to Americans who need it, **more than 1.5 million Americans have signed up for healthcare this year alone.**

We can't let the GOP gut health care. That's why Joe needs you to have his back. **[Will you chip in $5 to make sure we can keep fighting for affordable health care?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c253b/7377f838/35bdda87/51fa593c/1153757341/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-taxsurveykicker?refcode=20171127.2nd&amount=10")**

Keep up the fight,

Peter Hanscom
Campaign Manager
